pages.title: Global Skills Assessment | Measure 96 Workplace Skills
pages.meta_description: SHL's Global Skills Assessment measures 96 behavioral skills in 15 minutes. Objective data for onboarding, upskilling, reskilling, and workforce planning.
paragraphs: 96 behavioral skills measured in 15 minutes. Objective, consistent skills data you can use from hiring decisions to development conversations to internal mobility.

The SHL Global Skills Assessment gives organizations a consistent, science‑based view of behavioral skills across candidates and employees. It provides a shared language for understanding strengths, gaps, and the behaviors that drive performance.

Because it’s built on one universal framework , the assessment creates a unified picture of skills across your organization. You can compare people fairly, understand capability at scale, and build a stronger foundation for development, mobility, and long‑term workforce planning.
With one consistent framework before and after hire, you can use the SHL Global Skills Assessment in hiring, across your workforce, or both.
Include the SHL Global Skills Assessment in role-specific hiring to identify your top candidates.
Reuse their skills profiles at onboarding to guide targeted development from day one.
Give the SHL Global Skills Assessment directly to employees.
In 15 minutes per person, you gain organization-wide visibility into capabilities through detailed reporting.
The assessment measures all 96 skills in SHL’s Universal Competency Framework, giving you a complete view of the workplace behaviors that drive performance across roles.

In around 15 minutes, individuals compare short behavioral statements and select the ones most like them. This simple, forced‑choice format captures authentic workplace behavior while reducing response bias.

The assessment produces a range of reports tailored to your use case. Individuals receive development insights they can use for onboarding or growth. HR teams can view results for single candidates or aggregated groups, and—when used for selection—generate structured interview guides or role‑aligned talent profiles.

Alongside current strengths and gaps, the Reskilling Potential add‑on shows who has the willingness and ability to successfully upskill. This gives you a clearer view of who can grow into future roles, projects, and opportunities.
Normed with global samples representative across job levels and geographies to enable consistent comparisons
Built to support fair practices grounded in EEOC guidance for equal opportunity
Make decisions you can defend because you're using validated methods with documented job relevance

The Global Skills Assessment gives you a complete picture of your workforce’s behavioral skills. Our SHL AI Readiness Profile applies a focused lens to that data, revealing who’s ready to work effectively in an AI-powered environment and where to build capability next.
Make smarter hiring decisions with skills data that you can carry forward through the employee lifecycle.
Uncover skills gaps and build targeted development plans that align to business objectives.
Use objective skills data to match employees to the right internal opportunities, and vice versa.

pages.title: Cognitive Ability Tests | Cognitive Assessments
pages.meta_description: Measure candidates' potential and ability to learn, adapt, and succeed in any role with SHL's cognitive ability tests and assessments.
pages.hero_text: Measure candidates' potential and ability to learn, adapt, and succeed in any role with SHL's cognitive ability tests and assessments.
paragraphs: Measure potential to learn, adapt, and perform in any role with SHL Verify Interactive cognitive assessments.

Our pre-employment cognitive assessment starts by asking candidates a series of interactive questions organized around a central principle linked to something they are likely to encounter in the role.
Measures general mental ability , or G, and effectively measures candidates’ problem-solving and critical reasoning capabilities.

Measures candidates’ ability to draw logical conclusions and complete scenarios based on the information provided. Tasks include reading statements, then determining which subsequent options cannot be true or must be true.

Measures candidates’ ability to draw inferences from, and understand relationships between, various concepts. Tasks include working out the pattern in a set of images and identifying the correct next image.

Measures candidates’ ability to work with numbers and use appropriate mathematics in different situations. Tasks include solving word problems, performing numerical calculations, and interpreting charts and graphs.

SHL's Verify Interactive delivers precise scores indicating candidates' cognitive ability. These scores, along with other SHL talent acquisition data such as behavior and personality assessments, culminate in a well-rounded job score for each candidate. SHL's cognitive assessments aim to empower your teams to make fair and efficient talent choices, leading to business success.

pages.title: Universal Competency Framework (UCF)
pages.meta_description: Learn how the Universal Competency Framework (UCF), which provides a rational, consistent, and practical basis for understanding people’s behaviors, can help you.
pages.hero_text: Learn how the Universal Competency Framework (UCF), which provides a rational, consistent, and practical basis for understanding people’s behaviors, can help you.
paragraphs: Competencies are ‘sets of behaviors that are instrumental in the delivery of desired results’ (Bartram et al, 2002). In the business context they are behaviors that support the attainment of objectives within the context of that specific organization’s strategy. Competencies are sometimes also called ‘capabilities’ but irrespective of the term used, the focus is on ‘behaviors’ not technical knowledge.

The Universal Competency Framework (UCF) provides a rational, consistent, and practical basis for understanding people’s behaviors at work and their likelihood of succeeding in certain roles and environments. We constructed the UCF from our wide-reaching program of research on workplace behaviors, so it can be used to build competency models for all types of jobs or to map existing models onto a universal set of competency components.

We also provide a full suite of talent management tools and content to support the UCF including APTA™, our patented, customizable personnel psychological testing tool for directly measuring the UCF competencies most important for success in a role. Additional tools include questionnaire items, behavioral anchors, interview questions, and assessment exercises for employee selection and development. These provide the source materials for building assessment programs. Most importantly, each competency component is linked to our portfolio of assessments .

A competency framework allows organizations to align individual behaviors to group or corporate goals. It specifies the requirements for good performance in a given role, thereby facilitating end-to-end talent management practices, from identifying job-relevant competencies to engaging talent assessment tools to managing performance.
The competency framework can help you to easily understand the areas of strength and improvement within the whole organization across various departments and locations.
Statistical analysis was progressed to inform this structure and to ensure the competencies at all levels are discrete.
Our Science team is constantly innovating and pushing the boundaries of what is deemed possible.
Their latest innovation is Universal Competency Assessment that can measure all 96 components of Universal Competency Framework in just 15 minutes!
Learn more about their work in our new innovation powerhouse, SHL Labs .
Learn the science and structure behind the UCF framework and how organizations can use it to map all job requirements all across levels, job families, and roles-together with example applications.
The guide also includes best practices to thoughtfully implement, or review, competency frameworks, with seven key questions and practical tips to put them into action.
With our platform of pre-configured talent acquisition and talent management solutions, maximize the potential of your company’s greatest asset—your people.
bullet_points: 8 general competency factors at the highest level.
20 competency dimensions further explaining the general factors.
96 skills at the most granular level.
Defines critical behaviors for any role,
Is firmly linked to observable behaviors,
Is built on empirical evidence and extensive scientific research highlighting the most valid predictors of performance across roles,
Provides a common framework to evaluate behaviors, potential, and performance,
Has levels of behaviors to allow implementation across all levels and functions, and
Provides consistent measurement that enables meaningful validation.

pages.meta_description: With our Realistic Job Previews, candidates can self-assess their fit with the role and with your company through a set of tasks and culture-based questions.

bullet_points: Realistic job previews ask a short set of task and culture-based questions to help candidates self-assess their fit with this role and with your company. This helps you engage best-fit applicants, and allow them to consciously opt-in.

RJPs are packed with immersive, branded multimedia, including employee video endorsements and animation, which create appealing and contextual views of your business. Bolster your candidate advocacy with powerful, repeatable promotion.

RJP’s instant feedback is used for guidance, not overall scoring. By empowering applicants to opt out pre-application, you reduce the risks of shortlisting candidates ill-fitted to your business and save applicants and recruiters valuable time.

pages.title: SHL SVAR | Spoken Language Assessment for Hiring
pages.meta_description: SHL SVAR is a spoken language assessment that uses AI-driven scoring to deliver fast, consistent results for hiring at scale.
pages.hero_text: Assess genuine language skills across speaking, vocabulary, grammar, listening and comprehension — giving you a reliable signal you can trust in high-volume hiring.
paragraphs: Assess genuine language skills across speaking, vocabulary, grammar, listening and comprehension — giving you a reliable signal you can trust in high-volume hiring.
SHL SVAR is our automated spoken-language assessment designed to measure real communication ability.
It evaluates how candidates speak and converse to give you a clear, evidence‑based view of workplace‑ready communication.

SVAR supports 20+ languages and 7 accents , works on mobile and web , and takes just 12–15 minutes to complete. AI‑powered scoring delivers fast, consistent results so hiring teams can assess spoken‑language capability at scale.

SHL SVAR measures the essentials of spoken communication in one quick assessment; how clearly candidates speak, their pronunciation and fluency, how well they understand and respond to information, and how accurately they use vocabulary and grammar.
And because it captures spontaneous, real-time speech, you get a genuine view of a candidate's spoken ability rather than a rehearsed response.
Automated scoring delivers fast, consistent results, helping you screen high-volume and multilingual talent pools with confidence.
It’s designed to be simple for candidates and efficient for hiring teams.
The result: a fast, transparent and evidence‑based view of spoken language capability.
SHL SVAR combines globally recognized language standards with AI-driven speech analysis to deliver reliable, scalable spoken language assessment.
Clear communication leads to better experiences and stronger relationships
Shared language standards support smoother internal and external communication
Accurate assessment means hiring candidates with the right language skills from day one
Consistent language standards across international operations
SHL SVAR is built for roles where spoken communication directly impacts performance, customer experience, and service quality.
SHL SVAR helps teams quickly identify candidates who can communicate clearly, confidently, and effectively from day one.

Fill your talent pools with the skills of the future to ensure your workforce can flex as their environments change. Find candidates at scale that will quickly make an impact and grow with your organization.

Accurately assess critical skills such as customer service, communication and language capabilities to identify candidates with the right skills and fit for BPO roles – while managing high volumes and controlling costs.

Customer care roles can be demanding and it takes a specific skillset to succeed. Identify candidates with the skills to deliver excellent customer service and adapt in a fast-changing contact center environment.
bullet_points: Step 1: Candidates listen to a sentence, prompt, or spoken information.
Step 2: They repeat or respond in their own words.
Step 3: AI models analyze pronunciation, fluency, grammar, vocabulary, and comprehension.
Step 4: Hiring teams receive a clear report showing performance across key language metrics.
Developed using globally sourced Common European Framework (CEFR)-aligned data and rated by CEFR experts
Uses AI-driven speech and language models
Validated on a diverse datasets with strong AI-to-expert correlation
Informed by multiple validation studies designed to support inclusivity and fairness
Customer service and contact centres
Technical support and helpdesks
Telesales and inside sales
Front‑office and client‑facing roles
Multilingual or global hiring programmes
